chemistry.scope_summary: Addition of various alcohols (isopropanol, 3-pentanol, cyclopentanol) to three different furanones (1a: H, 1b: rac-OEt, 1c: (-)-OMent) yielding products in 57-94% yields. Also demonstrated [2+2] photocycloaddition of furanone 1a with alkenes under UVC light.

optimization_table.key_findings: DMBP concentration of 6.7 mM was optimal, complete reactions achieved with residence time of 5 min

figures.caption: Fig. 1. Microreactor setups: (a) Micronit Microchip with UV-LED array; (b) dwell device (Mikroglas Chemtech) under a UVA panel; and (c) dual microcapillary tower with central UVA fluorescent tube. | Scheme 1. 4,4 0 -Dimethoxybenzophenone (DMBP)-sensitized isopropa- nol addition to furanones.
Fig. 2. Multi-microcapillary flow reactor (M m CFR).

figures.key_information: Batch reactor setup with heating mantle, round bottom flask, mechanical stirring, and reflux condenser
figures.caption: Scheme 2. [2 þ 2] Photocycloaddition of furanone  1a  with alkenes. | Fig. 3. UVC-microreactor. (a) Setup with inserted microcapillary unit. (b) Microcapillary unit. | Chart 1. 3    3 library synthesized in parallel (conditions:[ 1 ]  ¼  33.3mM; [DMBP] ¼  6.7 mM; volume  ¼  15 mL; flow rate: 0.5 and 0.25 mL min  1 ) (DMBP, 4,4 0 -dimethoxybenzophenone).

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous-flow microreactors offer major advantages for photochemical applications, providing faster conversions, improved product qualities, and higher yields compared to conventional batch processes
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 300 min to 5 min residence time, light source power reduced from 64W to 36W
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photosensitizer (DMBP), same substrate types, same reaction mechanism

figure_descriptions: UVC-microreactor setup and batch reactor scheme

conflicts: Light power reported as 64W for batch and 36W for flow - different setups may explain discrepancy
notes: Data extracted from a review paper focusing on photochemical reactions in flow. Some fields are null due to review nature rather than original research paper with complete experimental details.
